fix: enforce scope-based tool filtering and improve error messages

Issue 1: Add scopeFilter metadata to ToolDefinition and SkillDefinition
interfaces. Tag each tool with 'repo', 'workspace', or 'both' scope.
Filter tool declarations in the stream route based on chatScope,
while preserving agent-selected tools (enabledToolIds override scope).

Issue 2: Replace silent catch blocks in actions.ts with proper error
messages. Functions like deletePrompt, updatePrompt, moveFile, etc.
now return { success: false, message } with the actual error instead
of silently swallowing exceptions.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

refactor: centralize AI model factory, replace direct Gemini instantiations

Create src/lib/aiModelFactory.ts with generateAIText() and generateAIContent()
helpers that respect AI_CONFIG.provider setting. When provider is
'github-copilot', routes through Copilot SDK; falls back to Gemini otherwise.

Refactored 11 functions in actions.ts that were directly instantiating
GoogleGenerativeAI, bypassing the provider abstraction:
- generateSystemPrompt, generateMagicContent, generateMagicSuggestions
- generateSuggestions, review agent delegation, extractReceiptInfo
- summarizeFile, synthesizeDocuments, extractTextFromImage
- suggestStrategies, processAgentJob (workerModel creation)

The chatWithAI Gemini fallback path is preserved as-is since it uses
the full Gemini chat session with function calling (already has its
own Copilot branch above it).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

- Hoist tool tracking vars to executeStream scope so catch block can
  access them for session.idle recovery
- Add skill execution bridge in executeAction() so SKILLS_LIBRARY
  entries (e.g. receipt_intelligence) route through executeSkill()
- Add file attachment/drag-drop into chat composer with upload support
- Move tool metrics recording from unreliable SDK events to executeTool
  callback for accurate tool names in timeline
- Add hydrateToolArgsWithContext() to inject fileIds into skill calls
- Add approval replay flow that replays blocked request with allow flags
- Streamline agent prompts and feedback loop engine

Meeting Planner V3: slider, AM/PM, periods, always visible

Major UX overhaul based on user feedback:

1. Step 2 always visible (not hidden until 2+ cities)
2. Time slider (0-23:30) — drag to explore meeting times,
   shows each city's local time with morning/day/evening/night
   color-coded cards as you drag
3. Timeline uses morning 🌅/day ☀️/evening 🌆/night 🌙 periods
   instead of just business/extended/off — more realistic for
   global teams where 'perfect overlap' rarely exists
4. AM/PM labels on timeline (12a, 3a, 6a, 9a, 12p, 3p, 6p, 9p)
5. Hover tooltips show '3 PM in London — ☀️ Daytime'
6. Calendar date picker styled for dark theme

- integer -> long, number -> double (was double for both)
- format: date-time -> DateTimeOffset, uuid -> Guid, duration -> TimeSpan
- Add MillisecondsTimeSpanConverter for TimeSpan JSON serialization
- Emit [Range], [RegularExpression], [Url], [MinLength], [MaxLength]
- Emit [StringSyntax(Uri)], [StringSyntax(Regex)], [Base64String]
- Change all public collections from concrete to interface types
  (List<T> -> IList<T>, Dictionary<K,V> -> IDictionary<K,V>)
- Lazy-initialize collection properties via field ??= pattern

commit.message:
feat: event-driven Now Playing with MISSED grace period (#175)

* fix: countdown timer flicker and accuracy

Server now renders 'Ns' directly when ≤30s remaining instead of
'less than a minute', eliminating the flip-flop between server-rendered
text and JS countdown. JS tick() runs immediately on page load instead
of waiting 1s for the first setInterval callback.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: dashboard live countdown and seconds in activity timestamps

Replace full page reload with in-place DOM updates for the Now Playing
countdown timer. The 5s poll now syncs remaining_seconds from the API
and a 1s ticker interpolates between polls, so the countdown never
resets. Structural changes (new schedules, device status, activity)
still trigger a full reload.

Add seconds to Recent Activity and History timestamps so short clips
no longer appear to start and end at the same time.

Bump version to 1.17.5.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: recompute remaining_seconds fresh in API to prevent countdown jumps

The scheduler tick runs every 15s, so remaining_seconds in _now_playing
can be up to 15s stale. The dashboard JS polls every 5s and syncs from
this value, causing the countdown to jump back to a higher number.

Fix: store start_time_raw and end_time_raw in _now_playing, then
recompute remaining_seconds at request time in dashboard_json()
using the current server time. This gives the JS countdown a fresh
baseline on every poll.

Also bumps version to 1.17.6.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: fresh remaining_seconds on page render + reduce reload triggers

Two root causes of countdown jumping:

1. Initial page render used stale remaining_seconds from the scheduler
   (15s tick). Now recomputes fresh in both dashboard() and
   dashboard_json().

2. Structural fingerprint was too sensitive — activity_count and
   starting/mismatch status flags caused unnecessary page reloads
   that reset the JS countdown. Removed transient fields from the
   fingerprint so reloads only happen for truly structural changes
   (different schedules, device list, pending/orphaned/offline).

Also adds a past-time-window warning when saving a schedule whose
end date is today but end time has already passed.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: add seconds to data-utc timestamp formatter in app.js

The client-side JS in app.js overwrites all [data-utc] elements with
toLocaleTimeString using only hour+minute, stripping the seconds that
the server-side Jinja2 template renders. Adding second: '2-digit' to
the formatter options fixes the display.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: restore starting/mismatch status in structural fingerprint

The starting and mismatch flags need to trigger reloads so the
badge transitions are visible (Starting -> Playing, mismatch
detection). These cause at most one reload per transition, unlike
activity_count which caused repeated reloads.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: match number input height to time input height

Both input[type=time] and input[type=number] now use 0.6rem vertical
padding so loop count and priority fields align with start/end time.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: event-driven Now Playing with MISSED grace period

Refactor Now Playing from scheduler-driven to device event-driven:

Protocol:
- Add PLAYBACK_STARTED/PLAYBACK_ENDED message types and Pydantic models

WebSocket handler:
- Handle PLAYBACK_STARTED: look up schedule, build now_playing entry, log STARTED
- Handle PLAYBACK_ENDED: clear now_playing, log ENDED

Scheduler:
- Remove _now_playing management and STARTED/ENDED logging
- Add 60s grace period before logging MISSED (prevents false positives from
  brief network hiccups)
- Track _offline_since per (schedule_id, device_id) combo
- Clean up _now_playing for disconnected devices
- Export set_now_playing/clear_now_playing/log_schedule_event as public API

Schedule deletion:
- Clear _now_playing entries immediately when schedule deleted

Tests:
- Rewrite TestNowPlayingCleanup for event-driven model (5 tests)
- Rewrite test_now_playing_has_remaining_seconds as set_now_playing test
- Add TestMissedGracePeriod (3 tests: before grace, after grace, reconnect)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* chore: bump version to 1.18.0

* fix: e2e test uses 23:59 end_time to avoid CI time-of-day failure

The TestScheduleEditWithDates._setup_schedule used end_time 17:00.
When CI runs after 17:00 UTC, 'today at 17:00' is in the past, so the
'in the past' warning modal correctly appears and the test fails.
Change to 23:59 so the schedule is almost always in the future.

* fix: remove exact version tag from scheduled daily builds

Scheduled daily builds were incorrectly being tagged with the full
version number (e.g., 2.0.7). Daily builds should only receive:
- latest
- short SHA
- major version (e.g., 2)
- major.minor version (e.g., 2.0)

- Pushes to main branch
- Manual workflow_dispatch runs

commit.message:
fix: parse session time and tokens from new CLI stats format (#83)

## Problem

PR #82 shows stats as `💰 1 premium · 🧠 high` — missing elapsed time (⏱️)
and token usage (🤖/📊).

The Copilot CLI changed its stats output format and we only fixed the
premium requests regex, not the time/model lines:

| Field | Old format | New format |
|-------|-----------|------------|
| Premium | `Total usage est: 3 Premium` | `Requests 1 Premium (15m
44s)` |
| Time | `Total session time: 15m 44s` | Embedded in Requests line above
|
| API time | `API time spent: 6m 29s` | Gone |
| Tokens | `gpt-5.4 5.5m in, 34.0k out` | `Tokens ↑ 5.5m • ↓ 34.0k •
5.3m (cached)` |

## Changes

- `_parse_stats()`: extract `session_time` from parenthesized time on
the Requests line, capture `tokens_line` from new Tokens line
- `CLIResult`: added `tokens_line` field
- `_format_stage_stats()`: shows 📊 tokens_line when old-format model
breakdown isn't available
- `review_pr()`: passes `tokens_line` through result dict

**Expected output after fix:**
```
💰 1 premium · ⏱️ 6m 16s · 🧠 high · 📊 ↑ 1.1m • ↓ 18.9k • 1.0m (cached) • 11.7k (reasoning)
```

150 tests pass (4 new).

Co-authored-by: Colin Cheung <11246116+ColinCee@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: countdown timer flicker and accuracy (#173)

* fix: countdown timer flicker and accuracy

Server now renders 'Ns' directly when ≤30s remaining instead of
'less than a minute', eliminating the flip-flop between server-rendered
text and JS countdown. JS tick() runs immediately on page load instead
of waiting 1s for the first setInterval callback.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: dashboard live countdown and seconds in activity timestamps

Replace full page reload with in-place DOM updates for the Now Playing
countdown timer. The 5s poll now syncs remaining_seconds from the API
and a 1s ticker interpolates between polls, so the countdown never
resets. Structural changes (new schedules, device status, activity)
still trigger a full reload.

Add seconds to Recent Activity and History timestamps so short clips
no longer appear to start and end at the same time.

Bump version to 1.17.5.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: recompute remaining_seconds fresh in API to prevent countdown jumps

The scheduler tick runs every 15s, so remaining_seconds in _now_playing
can be up to 15s stale. The dashboard JS polls every 5s and syncs from
this value, causing the countdown to jump back to a higher number.

Fix: store start_time_raw and end_time_raw in _now_playing, then
recompute remaining_seconds at request time in dashboard_json()
using the current server time. This gives the JS countdown a fresh
baseline on every poll.

Also bumps version to 1.17.6.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: fresh remaining_seconds on page render + reduce reload triggers

Two root causes of countdown jumping:

1. Initial page render used stale remaining_seconds from the scheduler
   (15s tick). Now recomputes fresh in both dashboard() and
   dashboard_json().

2. Structural fingerprint was too sensitive — activity_count and
   starting/mismatch status flags caused unnecessary page reloads
   that reset the JS countdown. Removed transient fields from the
   fingerprint so reloads only happen for truly structural changes
   (different schedules, device list, pending/orphaned/offline).

Also adds a past-time-window warning when saving a schedule whose
end date is today but end time has already passed.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: add seconds to data-utc timestamp formatter in app.js

The client-side JS in app.js overwrites all [data-utc] elements with
toLocaleTimeString using only hour+minute, stripping the seconds that
the server-side Jinja2 template renders. Adding second: '2-digit' to
the formatter options fixes the display.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: restore starting/mismatch status in structural fingerprint

The starting and mismatch flags need to trigger reloads so the
badge transitions are visible (Starting -> Playing, mismatch
detection). These cause at most one reload per transition, unlike
activity_count which caused repeated reloads.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: match number input height to time input height

Both input[type=time] and input[type=number] now use 0.6rem vertical
padding so loop count and priority fields align with start/end time.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
